title: Discuss & Draft
subtitle: Draft your priorities for elected representatives — refine with AI if you want, check a scope, and save.

steps.title: What Is a Mandate?
steps.description:
A mandate is your instruction to your elected representatives — what you want them to fight for.

Unlike a poll or petition, a mandate is your own words. It's tied to your location so it reaches the right representatives at the right level: federal (Congress), state (legislature), or town (local officials).

Mandates are aggregated into a public summary — The People's Pulse — so representatives can see what their constituents actually want.

steps.title: Where to Draft
steps.description:
The Discuss & Draft workspace appears on several pages:

1. Talk (/talk/) — The main civic hub. Scoped by geography:
   - /talk/ — federal scope (USA)
   - /talk/?state=7 — state scope (e.g. Connecticut)
   - /talk/?town=119 — town scope (e.g. Putnam)

2. The Fight (/elections/the-fight.php) — Elections action page, scoped to federal.

3. Town pages (e.g. /putnam/) — Scoped to your town.

The scope checkbox matching the page is pre-checked for you.

steps.title: The Prompt Box
steps.description:
At the top you'll see a text box with three buttons:

- Add — drops your text directly into the draft workspace as a bubble. No AI involved.
- Include AI — sends your text to AI for refinement. Both your text and the AI response appear as separate bubbles.
- Microphone — tap to dictate by voice. Say "command" to switch to voice command mode.

Press Enter to Include AI, or click Add if you want to draft without AI help.

steps.title: The Draft Workspace
steps.description:
Below the prompt is the Discuss & Create Draft box. Every bubble you create lands here, newest on top.

Each bubble shows:
- A draft ID (#1, #2, etc.)
- Who wrote it (You or AI)
- A timestamp
- Edit (pencil) and Delete (x) buttons

You can go back and forth with AI — add your idea, include AI to refine it, edit the result, keep iterating until you're happy.

steps.title: Check a Scope and Save
steps.description:
When a bubble is ready, check one of four scope boxes:

- Federal — saves to your U.S. congressional representatives
- State — saves to your state legislators
- Town — saves to your local town officials
- Idea — saves privately (not published)

Only one scope can be checked at a time. Checking a scope reveals a gold Save button. Click Save to confirm — the bubble is removed from the workspace and appears in the Public Mandate Summary below.

steps.title: The People's Pulse
steps.description:
All saved mandates feed into The People's Pulse — a public summary dashboard.

The Pulse shows:
- Topic breakdown — which policy areas your community cares about most
- Citizen voices — sample mandates in people's own words
- Activity trends — this week vs. last week
- Geo scope tabs — switch between Federal, State, and Town views
- CSV export — download the full dataset

This is the aggregated voice of your district — what representatives should be hearing.

steps.title: Tips
steps.description:
- Be specific: "Pass the DISCLOSE Act" is stronger than "fix campaign finance"
- One idea per mandate: keep each mandate focused on a single action
- Use Add for quick drafts, Include AI when you want help refining
- Edit any bubble before saving — click the pencil icon
- The scope checkbox is pre-checked based on the page you're on
- Your mandates are tied to your verified location — they reach the right representatives
- Voice commands: say "save federal #3" or "delete #2" in command mode
- Clear All wipes the workspace — saved mandates are safe in the summary below
